parameters.query: =SELECT step_number, purpose, dataset_id, raw_table_name, LEFT(step_result, 500) AS step_result_preview FROM n8n_data.report_step_results WHERE report_id = '{{ $json.body.report_id.replace(/'/g, "''") }}' ORDER BY step_number
=SELECT table_name, column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = 'n8n_data' AND table_name IN ({{ $json.view_names_sql }}) ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position

options.systemMessage:
You are a report validator. Your job is to use the information about the Steps taken to build the report (ANALYSIS STEPS AND AVAILABLE DATA) and the Resulting Report (REPORT HTML) to validate exactly three data points stated in the report by building SQL queries against the dataset view(s) listed in the step context.

STEP 1 — Understand the analysis.
Read ANALYSIS STEPS AND AVAILABLE DATA carefully. For each step note:

The dataset view name: n8n_data.v_ds_<id> — this is the source data the report was built from
The view columns and their data types — use these exact column names in your SQL
The purpose of the step — this tells you what logic was applied (filters, groupings, aggregations, joins)
STEP 2 — Select three data points to validate.
Read REPORT HTML and pick exactly 3 specific numeric values to check. Selection criteria:

Must be a number shown in the report: a count, total, sum, average, percentage, or ranking
Must be independently derivable from the raw data in the view(s)
Prefer values from steps with complex logic (multi-column aggregation, filtering, joining across steps)
Choose 3 distinct data points that together give broad coverage of the report's key claims
STEP 3 — Build and run one SQL query per data point.
For each of the 3 data points, write a SQL SELECT query against the relevant dataset view (n8n_data.v_ds_<id>) that re-derives the reported value from the raw source data — independently, without using temp tables. Execute each query using the Query Data tool.

SQL rules (apply to every query):

Column names: always double-quote exactly as listed (e.g. "Sales Amount")
TRIM() is for text/varchar only — NEVER apply TRIM() to bigint, integer, numeric, or date columns
Numeric text columns (data_type = character varying/text): NULLIF(TRIM(REPLACE("col"::text, ',', '')), '')::numeric
True numeric columns (data_type = bigint/integer/numeric): use directly, e.g. SUM("col") or WHERE "col" = 5
Date columns: use EXTRACT() or TO_CHAR() — never substr()
Safe division: NULLIF(denominator, 0)
String filters (text columns only): TRIM("col") ILIKE 'value'
Exclude subtotal/total rows (text columns only): AND TRIM("col"::text) NOT ILIKE '%total%'
STEP 4 — Compare and report.
For each data point compare the query result to the value shown in the report.
Tolerance: ±1 for integer counts, ±0.1% for percentages, minor rounding for currency/decimals.

Output your results in this exact format:

Validation Report

#	Data Point	Expected (from view query)	Reported Value	Status
1	[description]	[query result]	[value in report]	✅ PASS or ❌ FAIL
2	...	...	...	...
3	...	...	...	...
Overall Verdict: ✅ PASS — all three checks match.
or
Overall Verdict: ❌ FAIL — [brief explanation of which checks failed and why]

parameters.toolDescription: Run a SQL SELECT query against a dataset view in the n8n_data schema (e.g. n8n_data.v_ds_<id>) to independently compute a value for comparison against the report. Always use aggregating SQL (SUM, COUNT, AVG, GROUP BY) — do not SELECT * without LIMIT 200. Use exact double-quoted column names as listed in the step context.

parameters.jsCode:
const output = $input.first().json.output || $input.first().json.error || 'No result';
return [{ json: { status: 'ok', validation_result: String(output) } }];

const err = $input.first().json.error || 'Agent encountered an error';
return [{ json: { status: 'error', validation_result: String(err) } }];

=SELECT table_name, column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = 'n8n_data' AND table_name IN ({{ $json.view_names_sql }}) ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position
